cpr: If the person becomes unresponsive and stops breathing, start CPR immediately.
choking: Clear the airway — alternate 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts until object is expelled.
bleeding: Apply firm direct pressure and hold for at least 10 minutes without releasing.
anaphylaxis: Use the EpiPen immediately and call 911 — antihistamines alone will not stop anaphylaxis.
stroke: Use FAST: Face droop, Arm weakness, Speech slurred, Time to call 911 now.
heart_attack: Sit the person upright, give aspirin if not allergic, and call 911.
drowning: Begin rescue breathing first — 5 initial breaths before starting chest compressions.
electric_shock: Do not touch the victim until the power source is confirmed off.
poisoning: Call Poison Control immediately and do not induce vomiting without instruction.
opioid_overdose: Administer naloxone (Narcan) and perform rescue breathing until it takes effect.
chest_wound: Seal a sucking chest wound with a three-sided dressing to allow air to escape.
spinal_injury: Hold the head still in the position found and do not move the person.
fracture: Immobilize the limb in its current position while controlling any bleeding.
sprain_dislocation: Apply ice, compress, and elevate the joint — do not attempt to relocate a dislocation.
head_injury: Monitor consciousness closely and call 911 if confusion, vomiting, or unequal pupils appear.
impaled_object: Stabilize the object with padding around it — never pull it out.
abdominal_injury: Cover protruding organs with a moist cloth and do not push them back in.
burns: Cool with running water for 20 minutes — do not apply ice, butter, or creams.
eye_injury: Flush chemicals with water for at least 20 minutes; do not rub or remove embedded objects.
dental_injury: Store knocked-out tooth in milk and see a dentist within 30-60 minutes.
hypothermia: Rewarm gradually with dry insulation; do not rub the skin or give alcohol.
frostbite: Rewarm in 37-40 degree C water only — do not rub frostbitten tissue.
heat_stroke: Cool aggressively with ice on neck, armpits, and groin — do not delay cooling.
lightning: Touch the victim immediately — lightning victims carry no residual charge; start CPR if needed.
altitude_sickness: Descend at least 500 meters immediately — do not continue ascending with any symptoms.
seizure: Do not restrain or put anything in the mouth — protect the head and time the seizure.
diabetic: Give 15-20 g of fast sugar if conscious; call 911 if unconscious or no improvement.
asthma: Sit upright in tripod position, use rescue inhaler, call 911 if no improvement after 10 puffs.
shock: Lay flat, elevate legs if no spinal or chest injury, keep warm, and call 911.
fainting: Lay flat, elevate legs, and do not let them stand up until fully alert.
bites_and_stings: Immobilize a snakebite limb below heart level; do not cut or suck the wound.
pediatric_emergency: Use 2 fingers for infant CPR — back blows and chest thrusts only for infant choking.
out_of_domain: This question is outside the scope of first-aid assistance.
panic_attack: Grounding and box breathing for acute panic episodes.
suicidal_crisis: Ask directly, stay present, call 988 or 911 if immediate danger.
acute_stress_reaction: Psychological first aid after trauma — safe, calm, grounded presence.
aggressive_outburst: De-escalate with calm voice and space; call 911 if violent.
bear_encounter: Do not run. Play dead for grizzlies; fight back against black bears.
animal_attack: Do not run or turn your back. Make yourself large and fight back.
lost_in_wilderness: Stop, stay calm, and signal for rescue — shelter before water before food.
water_sourcing: Boil or purify all wild water. Dark urine and dizziness signal dehydration.
poisonous_plant: Wash plant contact with soap and water within 10 minutes. Do not rub eyes.
avalanche: Create an air pocket immediately and spit to find which way is down.
wildfire: Move perpendicular to the fire, protect your airway, and never run uphill into flames.
